description: Antibiotic resistance - mechanisms, spread, alternative therapies.

facts.context: Resistance genes spread between bacteria primarily via:
distractors: only vertical inheritance from parent to offspring cells
spontaneous mutations occurring independently in each cell
environmental chemical exposure causing genetic changes
broadly stimulating and enhancing the human host immune response non-specifically
universally blocking all fundamental bacterial metabolic processes without specificity
indiscriminately degrading DNA sequences in all nearby cells regardless of type

facts.truth: high specificity and ability to co-evolve with bacteria
distractors: broader spectrum activity than chemical antibiotics
complete absence of resistance development ever
no regulatory approval requirements needed

facts.context: The antibiotic discovery gap since 1980s results from:
facts.truth: low return on investment and difficult target identification
distractors: all possible antibiotics already being discovered
bacteria no longer developing any new resistance
regulatory agencies blocking all new research

facts.context: Efflux pump resistance mechanisms work by:
facts.truth: actively pumping antibiotics out of bacterial cells
distractors: preventing antibiotics from entering cells at all
chemically modifying antibiotics to inactive forms
producing enzymes that degrade the antibiotic

facts.context: Biofilm infections are harder to treat because:
facts.truth: matrix protects bacteria and reduces antibiotic penetration
distractors: biofilm bacteria are genetically different species
antibiotics work better on biofilm than planktonic cells
biofilms only form on non-living surfaces

facts.truth: they preserve beneficial microbiome and reduce resistance selection
distractors: they are cheaper to manufacture than broad-spectrum
they work faster than broad-spectrum antibiotics
all infections can be treated with narrow-spectrum

facts.truth: optimize use to slow resistance while maintaining efficacy
distractors: eliminate all antibiotic use completely
only reduce healthcare costs not resistance
increase antibiotic use for better outcomes

facts.context: Persister cells survive antibiotic treatment by:
facts.truth: entering dormant state not through genetic resistance
distractors: having mutations that inactivate antibiotics
actively pumping out all antibiotics rapidly
not existing as they are a theoretical concept

facts.context: Combination antibiotic therapy can help by:
facts.truth: reducing resistance emergence through multiple targets
distractors: always being more toxic than monotherapy
never providing any advantage over single drugs
only being used when resistance already exists

facts.context: Agricultural antibiotic use contributes to resistance by:
facts.truth: selecting resistant bacteria that can transfer to humans
distractors: having no effect on human-relevant resistance
only affecting resistance in plant pathogens
being banned worldwide already preventing issues

facts.context: Antimicrobial peptides as alternatives face challenges including:
distractors: complete lack of any demonstrable antibacterial efficacy in laboratory studies
the fundamental impossibility of bacteria ever developing peptide resistance mechanisms
extremely low production costs that undermine commercial pharmaceutical viability
